■ Camera settings and connect to PC
■ Installation of device driver
■ Installation of some necessary packages
■ USB setting
1.Sample Application Build
■ Conditions
■ Steps
2.Sample Application Usage
■ Steps

3. Note
■ Character code

0. Preparation [OS common]
■Camera settings and connect to PC(/SBC). (1/7)
[Wired LAN (1/4)]
WiredLAN uses ILCE-1 and ILME-FX6 as examples. For other
models, refer to "PC Remote Function" in the help guide.
1.Please set “On” for “Network > Wired LAN > Wired
LAN Connect” Menu and set “On” for “Network >
Cnct./Remote Sht. > Remote Shoot Function > Remote
Shooting” Menu.
ILCE-1
2.Please connect the
camera and your
PC(/SBC)
directly (or through
LAN Hub / LAN
Router) with an
Ethernet cable.
3. If you want to connect the camera and host with a
fixed IP address, set “Manual” for “IP Address setting”
in “Wired LAN Setting”.
If you want to use the DHCP service of the router to
automatically assign an
IP address, set "Auto" in
"IP Address Settings".
ILCE-1

0. Preparation [OS common]
■Camera settings and connect to PC(/SBC). (2/7)
[Wired LAN (2/4)]
⚫Host setting requirement by the combination of
connection type and ”IP Address setting”.
Direct Use HUB Use Router
IP Address Setting Auto Manual Auto Manual Auto Manual
Windows *1 - *1 - *1 -
macOS *2
PC Linux *3 - *3 - *4 -
Jetson Nano *3 - *3 - *4 -
Raspberry Pi 2/4 -
*1 Enable network discovery and file sharing when using a
Windows account without administrative privileges
*2 When Firewall is ON, allow connections by applications in
the following way:
Open Firewall Options (System Preferences > Security &
Privacy > Firewall > Firewall Options...)
Set "Allow incoming connections" for the applications
*3 Set the network setting to "Link Local Only"
*4 Set the network setting to "Automatic (DHCP)"

0. Preparation [OS common]
■Camera settings and connect to PC(/SBC). (3/7)
[Wired LAN (3/4)]
ILCE-1
4. Please enter IP
address, Subnet Mask,
Default Gateway, and
DNS Settings for "IP
address setting".
5. Wait for about 30 seconds to connect to the Ethernet
cable and for the camera to recognize the network.
Then you are ready to connect to the host PC(/SBC).
6. For a Wired LAN connection, you need to execute
"Pairing" between the camera and the host PC(/SBC).
To perform "Pairing", you need to send a Connect
request from the application to the camera and
operate the screen on the camera.
The pairing procedure is explained in "Sample
Application Usage → ■ Steps" on p.17.

0. Preparation [OS common]
[ILME-FX6/MPC-2610]
■Camera settings and connect to PC(/SBC). (4/7)
[Wired LAN (4/4)]
ILME-FX6
4. Please enter IP
address, Subnet
Mask, Gateway,
and etc. for
“Detail Settings".
5. Wait for about 30 seconds to connect to the Ethernet
cable and for the camera to recognize the network.
Then you are ready to connect to the host PC(/SBC).
6. Please also set up User Name and Input Password
for “Access Authentication”.
ILME-FX6

0. Preparation [OS common]
■Camera settings and connect to PC(/SBC). (5/7)
[Wireless LAN (1/2)]
WirelessLAN uses ILCE-1 as examples. For other models,
refer to "PC Remote Function" in the help guide.
- Use Wi-Fi Direct
1. Please executes “Network > Cnct./Remote Sht. >
Remote Shoot Function > Wi-Fi Direct Info” display
the SSID and password required for the user to
connect to the camera.
ILCE-1
If you leave the SSID and Password
displayed on the camera and search
for the SSID on the host PC, the SSID will be listed. You can also
connect directly to the camera by manually entering the SSID and
password above without searching for the SSID on the host PC.
2. Once the connection to the camera's SSID is
completed, “Remote Shooting” will change from “Off”
to “On”. When it changes to “On”, you will be able to
connect remotely.

0. Preparation [OS common]
■Camera settings and connect to PC(/SBC). (6/7)
[Wireless LAN (2/2)]
- Use router AP
1. Please executes “Network > Access Point Set.” Menu.
ILCE-1
2. Once you have decided on the
Access Point to connect to and
entered the password etc., you
are ready to connect.
3. Change “Wi-Fi Connect” from “Off” to “On” to connect
to the Access Point set in step 2.
4. When a connection is establis-
hed to the Access Point,
“Wi-Fi Connect” changes to “On”. When it changes to
“On”, you will be able to connect remotely.
#If you are having trouble connecting, please refer to
"Camera body settings for wireless LAN connection" in
the API specifications.

0. Preparation [OS common]
■Camera settings and connect to PC(/SBC). (7/7)
[USB]
USB connection uses ILCE-1 as examples. For other models,
refer to "PC Remote Function" in the help guide.
1. Please connect the camera and your PC(/SBC)
with a USB cable. When the camera recognizes the USB
cable, four options will be displayed on the “Select USB
Connect Mode”, so select “Remote Shoot(PC Remote)”.
Then "Network > Cnct./Remote Sht.
> Remote Shoot Function
> Remote Shooting" changes to "On"
ILCE-1
If you set the following menu, the confirmation screen will not be displayed next
time. "Setup > USB > USB Connection Mode"
[For Windows]
3.Please check
“Device Manager” if your
camera “ILCE-xxx” is
under “Portable Devices”.
xxx

0. Preparation [For Windows]
■Installation of device driver. (1/2)
1. Open “Driver” folder.
2. Select inf file and open menu at right click.
srcameradriver_dscrx0m2.inf : Use DSC-RX0M2.
srcameradriver.inf : Use other models.
3. Click “Install”.

0. Preparation [For Windows]
■Installation of device driver. (2/2)
4. Click “Yes” if you ask below.
5. Click “Install”.
6. Click “OK”.
7. Please check “Device Manager”
if “Sony Remote Control Camera”
is under “libusbK Usb Devices”.
8. If you come to this step,
you are ready to develop
your application
by using Camera Remote SDK.

0. Preparation [For Linux]
■Installation of some necessary packages.
1. Install some necessary packages below.
$ sudo apt install autoconf libtool libudev-dev gcc g++ make cmake unzip libxml2-dev
■USB setting
1. Change USB bulk setting
[Raspberry Pi OS]
Add the command below at the end of the file “/etc/rc.local” before "exit 0"
to modify Bulk Transfer Rate configuration file.
Add this command:
sudo sh -c 'echo 150 > /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb’
[Ubuntu (for Embedded)]
Change “APPEND ${cbootargs} quiet” to the command
below in the file “/boot/extlinux/extlinux.conf”.
Before:
APPEND ${cbootargs} quiet
After:
APPEND ${cbootargs} usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=150 usbcore.autosuspend=-1
[Ubuntu (for x86)]
Change “quiet splash” to the command
below in the file “/etc/default/grub” and update grub.
Before:
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="quiet splash“
After:
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="quiet splash usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=150"
sudo update-grub
2. Reboot and check the configuration
Save & Close the file and reboot.
Make sure that “150” is written in the configuration file.
“/sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb”.
$ cat /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb
150

Before:
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="quiet splash“
After:
GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="quiet splash usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=150"

1. Sample Application Build [For Windows]
■Conditions
1. “Visual Studio 2022 / 2019” are available
2. Windows SDK version 10.0 (and above) is needed → Fig. 5
3. CMake 3.21 (and above) is needed.
■Steps
1.Unzip the package file downloaded.
2.Make build folder and execute cmake.
if Visual Studio 2022: if Visual Studio 2019:
> mkdir build > mkdir build
> cd build > cd build
> cmake -A "x64" -T "v143,host=x64" .. > cmake -A "x64" -T "v142,host=x64" ..
＃ Reflected in the green frame in the figure below.
3.Open the solution file “..¥build¥RemoteCli.sln”
4.Build.
5.If an error requires “10.0.22621.0”
for Windows SDK version, please change
the setting in the project property
to the version you have in your environment. Fig. 5

1. Sample Application Build [For Linux]
■Conditions
See “Installation of some necessary packages.” on p.12
■Steps
1. Copy the package file downloaded to a work directory.
(such as “/home/user01/work/”).
2. Unzip the file.
$ cd /home/user01/work
$ unzip [the package file]
3. Make a build directory and execute cmake build.
$ cd /home/user01/work/[Sample Application unzipped directory]
$ mkdir build
$ cd build
$ cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release ..
$ cmake --build .
4. Execute Sample Application.
$ ./RemoteCli
# (If you use Sample Application without building in your
environment, you may need “chmod”.)
$ chmod +x RemoteCli

1. Sample Application Build [For macOS]
■Conditions
1. Xcode 14.1 is available.
# Xcode depends on the OS version, please follow the operating
requirements.
2. CMake 3.24.1 is needed.
■Steps (1/2)
1. Copy the package file downloaded to a work directory.
(such as “/Users/user01/work/”).
2. Unzip the file.
$ cd /Users/user01/work
$ unzip [the package file]
3. Make a build directory and execute cmake build.
$ cd /Users/user01/work/[Sample Application unzipped directory]
$ mkdir build
$ cd build
$ cmake –GXcode ..
4. Launch xcode with “RemoteCli.xcodeproj”
$ open RemoteCli.xcodeproj

1. Sample Application Build [For macOS]
■Steps (2/2)
5. Select “Build Configuration” to “Release” from the menu
“Product” – “Scheme” – “Edit Scheme ...” and close the menu.
6. Build Sample Application from the menu “Product” – “Build”.
7. Execute Sample Application.
$ cd Release
$ ./RemoteCli

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
The availability of each menu setting and execution
depends on the status of the camera. For more information,
please refer to the camera's menu status or help guide.
■Steps(1/2)
1.Firstly, please connect the camera to your PC(/SBC).
(As same settings in Page 3.)
2.Please find the RemoteCli Sample Application file under
the folder “..¥build¥Release”.
3.Please execute the file.
4.Console window will show up like below.
5. If you connect the camera via a wired LAN, "Pairing" is
required for the first time.
Select “Pairing” in “PC Remote
Function” to display the pairing
standby screen.
ILCE-1

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Steps(2/2)
6.Please choose the number and enter.
If you connect the camera via a wired LAN and are
waiting for "Pairing", the standby screen will change to the
next confirmation. Press "OK" to complete the pairing.
After "Pairing" is complete,
turn off the camera, wait
about 20 seconds, and then
turn it on.
# When you restart the camera, the pairing information will be
saved in the camera.
Allow at least 10 seconds before turning the power on again.
If you restart your camera before saving, you will need to
perform "Pairing" again.
＃The pairing information saved in the camera will be
discarded when the camera settings are reset. After resetting,
perform "Pairing" again.
#You can also connect without "Pairing".
If you set "Connect without Pairing"
ILCE-1
to "Enable", unintended third
parties may access the camera.
Sony is not liable for any problems
or damage caused by setting
"Connect without Pairing" to
"Enable".

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Top Menu
1.Console window shows menu like below.
2.Please choose a number/alphabet and enter.
#If you choose “x” and enter, the application closes.
#Not all models can connect in "Contents Transfer
Mode". The corresponding model name can be
found in the API specification.
3. On success to connect,
you will take the message like below.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
- SSH authentication connection
1. If the camera supports SSH authentication, the
fingerprint obtained from the camera is displayed.
If the fingerprint is correct, “y” and enter.
#If you choose “n” and enter, to go back to the menu.
2. Next, enter the password
# In the sample application, the User Name is fixed to
"admin“. Modify the sample application as needed.
3. On success to connect,
you will take the message like below.

1.When connected in "Remote Control Mode“ or “Remote

2.Please choose a number/alphabet and enter.

5.Please input “-1” and enter, to go back to the menu.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Status display and camera switching
1.Please input “s” and enter.
2.Please input “1” and enter.
3.Please choose the number and enter.
4.Please input “s” and enter,
to check the connection status.
5.Please input “-1” and enter, to go back to the menu.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Shutter/Rec Operation Menu
This menu is for still image shooting and movie recording.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Shutter Release
1.Please input “1” and enter.
2.Camera takes a photo and stores in the folder
set by “set_save_info()” in the source code.
# On success to take a photo,
you will take the message like below.
#If you cannot take a photo,
please try again after changing
mode dial to “M” and focus mode dial to “MF”.
focus mode dial “MF” mode dial “M”

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Shutter Half Release in AF mode
1.Please input “2” and enter.
2.Please input “y” and enter.
3.Camera only makes AF control with half release.
■Shutter Half and Full Release in AF mode
1.Please input “3” and enter.
2.Please input “y” and enter.
3.Camera makes AF control with half release
and Cameraｚ takes photo and stores in the folder
set by “set_save_info()” in the source code.
#If you cannot take a photo, please try again after
changing mode dial to “M” and focus mode dial to “AF-S”.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Continuous Shooting
1.Please input “4” and enter.
2.Camera takes several photos and stores in the
folder set by “set_save_info()” in the source code.
#If you cannot take a photo, please try again after
changing mode dial to “M” and focus mode dial to “MF”.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Display and change Focus Bracket Shot
1.Please input “5” and enter,
Change Still Capture Mode(Drive Mode) to FocusBracket
and shoot.
2.Camera takes several photos and stores in the
folder set by “set_save_info()” in the source code.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Movie Rec Button
1.Please input “6” and enter.
2.Then if you choose “y”,
console window shows candidate values.
3. Please choose “[2] Down”
to start movie rec(make movie rec button down).

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
[ILME-FX6/MPC-2610]
■Movie Rec Button(Toggle)
1.Please input “7” and enter.
2.Then if you choose “y”,
console window shows candidate values.
3.Please choose “[2] Down”
to start movie rec(make movie rec button down).
4. To stop movie rec, enter “[1] Up” (make movie rec
button up) and “[2] Down” (make movie rec button down).
# After sending “Down”, “Up” must always be sent.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Shooting Menu
This menu is for still image shooting and movie recording.

“PC Remote Setting” beforehand.
1.Please input “1” and enter.
2.Console window shows
current Still Capture Mode value.
3.Then if you choose “y”,
console window shows candidate values.
4.Please choose the number and enter.
～

■Zoom Operation(1/2)
Please note that Power Zoom Lens is needed to be
attached to the camera body for this operation.

3.Then if you choose “y”,
console window shows candidate values.
4.Please choose a number for zoom operation
or input zoom range value.

For models that can change the zoom type,

4.Please choose a number for zoom operation

Please note that Power Zoom Lens is needed to be

1.Please input “4” and enter.
2.Console window shows
slot numbers and operation(DeviceProperty)
names that can be selected.
3. Please choose the operation number and enter.
4.Then choose the slot number and enter.
# Above example: numbers from 0 to 4

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]

This menu is for Focus.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■ Display and change Focus Mode
1.Please input “1” and enter.
2.Console window shows current Focus Mode value.
3.Then if you choose “y”,
console window shows candidate values.
4.Please choose the number and enter.

1.Please input “2” and enter.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■AF Area Position(x,y)
1.Please input “3” and enter.
2.Sample Application will change focus area setting
to “Flexible Spot S” automatically.
3. Then if you choose “y”,
console window asks you to input x, y position
for the center of the focus frame.
4.Please input values.
(It is recommended to input values at the center of
camera view, such as 320, 240, for trial purpose.)

■Display and change Focus Position Setting
1.Please input “4” and enter.

current Focus Position value and settable range.

■Display and change Zoom Position Setting

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Setup Menu
This menu is for various settings.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Display and change DISP(Screen Disp) Setting
1.Please input “1” and enter.
2.Console window shows current DISP(Screen Disp)
Setting value.
3.Entering “y” switches to “Display All Information” mode if
the current value is not “Display All Information” mode,
otherwise switches to “Histogram” mode.
■Display and change Monitor LUT Setting
1.Please input “2” and enter.
2.Console window shows
current Monitor LUT Setting value.
3.Then if you choose “y”,
console window shows candidate values.
4.Please choose the number and enter.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Download the camera setting file to PC
1.Please input “3” and enter.
2.To save the downloaded data with the specified file
name, enter your file name after “file name >”
If you do not enter a file name, the data is to be
automatically saved as “Model name + CUMSET.DAT”.
# If the download fails, you will be notified of the
OnWarning callback.
# If the download is successful,
the OnCompleteDownload callback will inform you of
the saved file name.
The following is an example of a successful download
without specifying a name.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Upload the camera setting file to Camera
1.Please input “4” and enter.
2. A list of files in the current path that can be uploaded
will be displayed. Enter the number of the file you wish
to upload.
# The result of the upload is notified in the OnWarning
callback.
If the upload is successful, the camera will reboot itself.
After the reboot, "disconnected" and "connected" will
appear on the console.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Camera Setting Reset
1.Please input “5” and enter.
2.Enter “y” if you are willing to reset the settings.
# Entering “y” will restart the camera. Please be careful.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■ Remote Firmware Update (1/2)
1. Please input “6” and enter,
console window shows list Remote Firmware Update
menu.
2. Please input “1” and enter,
console window shows current camera firmware
version.
3. Please input “2” and enter,
then asked to enter target firmware path.
4. Please input the path and enter,
the following is the output when the precheck is OK.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■ Remote Firmware Update (2/2)
5. Please input “3” and enter,
then asked to enter target firmware path.
5. Please input the path and enter,
the following is the output when the upload complete.
6. Please input “4” and enter,
console window shows current upload progress of
firmware.
6. When you want to cancel firmware upload,
please input “5” and enter .
7.Then if you choose “y”, cancel firmware update.
8. Please input “6” and enter.
9. Then if you choose “y”, start firmware update.
10. Reconnect the camera after firmware update complete.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■ Check Firmware file info
1. Please input “7” and enter,
console window shows firmware version about
uploaded firmware file.
Always firmware version is 9.99 if debug mode is
“Pseudo_update_with_Fake_Firmware”
■ Debug Mode of firmware update
1. Please input “8” and enter,
console window shows current Debug Mode status.
2.Then if you choose “y”,
console window shows candidate values.
3.Please choose the number and enter.
# For details of setting values, please refer to the API
specification.

media.

6. Console window shows Contents Info List in selected
date.

■ Display content list and get content at “Remote
Transfer Mode” (2/2)

and enter.
1: Original content data.
2: Thumbnail data
3: Screennail data (Only support image contents)
2.Console window shows “Get Contents Data OK”.
3.You can find the file in the shown path.

■ Show Detail Info
1. Please input “4” and enter.
2. Console window shows
~

console window shows list Movie Playback menu.

console window shows Media slot number.
2.Please choose the number and enter.

media.

selected date.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■ Movie Playback Setting (3/3)
9.The following is the output when the set is successful.
10.Please input “2” and enter if you want to confirm set
parameter.
11.Console window shows set parameter.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■ Control Movie Playback
1. Please input “3” and enter,
console window shows list Control Movie Playback
menu.
1.Start: Need execute first.
Start Movie Playback operation.
2.Stop: Need execute finally.
Finish Movie Playback operation.
3.Play: Start play movie playback.
4.Pause: Stop movie playback temporary.
5.Seek: Move playback position.
The following is the output when the set is successful.
# This application is not support about show playback
image.
Please refer to the API specification.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■ Confirm Movie Playback Status
1. Please input “4” and enter,
the following is the output when the request is
successful.
The request is failed when movie playback operation is
not started.
2. Please input “5” and enter,
Console window shows current movie playback status.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Selected Media Format
1.Please input “6” and enter.

“Quick Format Enable Status” is Enabled.
# “Quick Format Enable Status” may not be
displayed for some models. In that case,
the format type selection is not displayed.
3.Then if you choose “y”,

please choose a number and enter.
6.Select “y” and enter, then format will start.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Display the character string of the camera body menu
(1/2)
#Please note that the camera must support the
GetDisplayStringList API in order to perform this operation.
1.Please input “7” and enter.
2.Console window shows candidate values.
3.Please choose the number and enter. For example, if
you want to request all information, input "0".
～
# The result of the request is notified in the OnWarning callback.
# Since the list is different for each model, it is not always
possible to get the information.
The following is the output when the request is successful.
When OnWarning notifies a success, enter "8" in Other-
Menu to confirm the list name that can be displayed.

■Display the character string of the camera body menu
(2/2)
4. Please input “8” and enter,
console window shows list names you can get.

output.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Monitoring (1/2)
1.Please input “10” and enter,
console window shows list Monitoring menu.
2.Then if you input “1” and enter,
asked to enter Monitoring conditions.
3. The only required field is the IP address of the hostPC.
Please enter and proceed.
# For details of setting values, please refer to the API
specification.
The following is the output when the set is successful.
If "Set NG" is displayed, change the value and try again.
After setting the conditions, use the (2) menu to start
monitoring.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]
■Monitoring (2/2)
4.Please input “2” and enter,
console window shows delivery status.
5.If the status is “False”, select “y”. Then start is executed.
If the request is accepted, “Request OK” is displayed.
6.To get the Live View image, enter “0” to return to
<< Other Menu >> and execute (1) Live View.
# If status is “True” and “y” is selected, the request is
rejected and “Request NG” is displayed.
# If the camera fails to start, the OnWarning callback
notifies the camera of the failure.
7.Please input “3” and enter,
console window shows delivery status.
8.If the status is “True”, select “y”. Then stop is executed.
If the request is accepted, “Request OK” is displayed.
# If status is “True” and “y” is selected, the request is
rejected and “Request NG” is displayed.

possible to get the information.
The following is the output when the request is successful.
When OnWarning notifies a success, enter “12” in Other-
Menu to confirm the list name that can be displayed.
2. Please input “12” and enter,
console window shows Zoom&Focus Preset information
you can get.
～

console window shows sub menu item.

2. If you want to set DateTime, Please Input “y”.
3. Then input date at you want to set and enter.
4. If you want to set Area, Please Input “y”.
5. Then input time difference at you want to set and enter.

2. Sample Application Usage [OS common]

6. If you want to set Daylight savings, Please Input “y”.
7. Then input Daylight saving status and enter.
If you want to set OFF, please input 0.
If you want to set ON, please input 1.
8. The following is the output when the set request is
successful.
Then the following is the output after set is successful.

successful.

notification will appear.

Console window shows result and current time zone
setting.

3. Note [OS common]
■Character code
There is a rare issue that no output will be displayed
in the sample application command window when you
use the different character code for your path name
from your pc character code setting.
